texto: Coming home to someone is many things. It is a literal action, an abstract idea, a physical feeling. It is more than the sound of the key turning in the door and the voice that calls from the porch. It is a choice, a promise, a declaration. It is a return, not as a person to a place, but as oneself to another. It is one person saying to another person: You are the one I choose.
explicacao: Citação de Tania De Rozario sobre temas ligados a familia. “Coming home to someone is many things. It is a literal action, an abstract idea, a physical feeli…” articula familia, family, home em registro de familia, com relevância para contextos como home e love.

frase_original: Coming home to someone is many things. It is a literal action, an abstract idea, a physical feeling. It is more than the sound of the key turning in the door and the voice that calls from the porch. It is a choice, a promise, a declaration. It is a return, not as a person to a place, but as oneself to another. It is one person saying to another person: You are the one I choose.

texto: I am a wall. I am a wall. I am a wall. I am a giant and I tower above you. I am a giant and I can't hear your voice. There is familiarity in this. I spent years like this growing up, my mother hovering over my every move, me responding monosyllabically, face blank, voice blank, heart blank. It is a coping mechanism and it is easy, if you are able to block out false promises of love with the understanding of what love has become.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Tania De Rozario: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “I am a wall. I am a wall. I am a wall. I am a giant and I tower above you. I am a giant and I can…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que familia, books, booksactually pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de inspiracao e booksactually.
